content: Gonorrhea/chlamydia: nucleic acid amplification tests (NAAT) from urine, vaginal, cervical, urethral, pharyngeal, or rectal swabs based on exposure sites; culture for antibiotic susceptibility testing if treatment failure. Syphilis: nontreponemal tests (RPR, VDRL) for screening/monitoring treatment response; treponemal tests (FTA-ABS, TP-PA, EIA/CIA) for confirmation; reverse algorithm uses treponemal screen first. HSV: PCR from lesion swab (preferred), viral culture (lower sensitivity), type-specific serology for prior exposure. Trichomonas: NAAT (most sensitive), wet mount (low sensitivity ~50%). Screening recommendations: sexually active women <25 annually for chlamydia/gonorrhea; MSM every 3-6 months; all adults at least once for HIV/syphilis; pregnant women comprehensive screening.
